titleStmt.title: Letter from the Fellow and Registrar, Royal College of Physicians of Ireland, to the Registrar, Pharmaceutical Society of Ireland, 5 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Fellow and Registrar, Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Typescript letter to the Registrar of The Pharmaceutical Society of Ireland about the sale of Carbolic Acid in Ireland, expressing the view of RCPI that the drug should be brought under the same regulations held in England. (See letter RCPI/2/3/2/13/143 and RCPI/2/3/1/58/T56)

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.persName: Fellow and Registrar, Royal College of Physicians of Ireland
correspAction.placeName: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland, Dublin, Ireland
Pharmaceutical Society of Ireland, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
RCP1/2/3/2/13/144 5th FEB. 16. The Registrar,
The Pharmaceutical Society of Ireland,
Dublin. Dear Sir, I am obliged for your letter of the 3rd inst., re
the sale of Carbolic Acid preparations for Agricultural
and Horticultural purposes. The President and Fellows of
the College carefully considered the matter yesterday
and came to the conclusion that is was advisable that
the sale of this drug for these purposes should be brought
under regulations similar to those which obtain in England. I remain, dear Sir,
Yours faithfully,
Fellow & Registrar.
